sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this shmup

special_mechanics.name: Biplane Flight
special_mechanics.description: Player controls a biplane that scrolls vertically through five levels. The plane navigates constantly scrolling backgrounds until reaching a runway. Movement is lateral with standard shooting and bomb weapons.

special_mechanics.name: Bomb Weapon
special_mechanics.description: Powerful explosive that clears a portion of the screen of bullets and damages enemies within blast radius. Player starts with 3 bombs, can collect 'B' icons to increase bomb stock.

special_mechanics.name: Scrolling Background
special_mechanics.description: Vertical scrolling background that never stops moving until runway is reached. Backgrounds scroll left and right for immersion.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Qualitative/Empirical data only. Source is Wikipedia article about Flying Shark arcade game (1987 original). Genesis port from 1990 has no specific movement physics documentation. Article describes gameplay mechanics and design philosophy but contains no quantified movement parameters, velocity values, gravity constants, or sub-pixel precision details. As a vertical shmup, traditional platformer physics (jump, gravity, friction) are not applicable.
